title: F-Tax Registration
description: Employers operating in Sweden must register for F-tax (F-skatt) with the Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket). F-tax status confirms that the company handles its own tax payments and is required for payroll processing.

title: GDPR — Swedish DPA (IMY) Registration for Special-Category Data
description: Processing special-category personal data (health, biometric, union membership, etc.) requires a lawful basis under GDPR Art. 9(2) and may trigger mandatory notification to the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (IMY). A Data Protection Officer (DPO) may be required.
trigger: Processing special-category data about Swedish residents

title: Employer Contributions (Arbetsgivaravgifter)
description: Swedish employers must pay social security contributions (arbetsgivaravgifter) on all employee remuneration. The standard rate is 31.42% of gross salary for employees under 65. Contributions fund the Swedish social insurance system.

title: Employment Protection Act (LAS) Compliance
description: The Swedish Employment Protection Act (Lagen om anställningsskydd) governs employment contracts, notice periods, and grounds for termination. Fixed-term contracts are restricted; general employment is presumed to be permanent unless otherwise agreed.
